LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AECOM ENVIRONMENTAL GROUP, INC, NICOLE TESTA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. WHILE DEFENDANT SUCCESSFULLY SHIFTED THE BURDEN ON EACH OF THE ELEMENTS FOR BOTH THE NEGLIGENCE AND MOTOR VEHICLE NEGLIGENCE CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON EACH OF THE ELEMENTS. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO THE ADMISSIBILITY OF THE NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL ACCIDENT REPORT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT DECLARATION OF MICHAEL J. MAHONEY IS OVERRULED. BOTH PARTIES' REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2012 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the court orders the balance of the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-15-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS AECOM ENVIRONMENTAL GROUP, INC, NICOLE TESTA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Nicole L. Jones of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (438.6663) for defendants Charter and Proctor; Robert E. Hayden and Michael Hale of L.M.S. (400.7000) for plaintiff and responding party; Michael D. McConathy of Lynch Gilardi & Grummer (397.2800) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied. While defendant successfully shifted the burden on each of the elements for both the negligence and motor vehicle negligence causes of action, plaintiff has presented triable issues of fact on each of the elements. Plaintiff's objection to the admissibility of the Nevada Highway Patrol accident report is overruled. Defendant's objection to plaintiff's expert declaration of Michael J. Mahoney is overruled. Both parties' request for attorney fees is denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS FALCON CABLE SYSTEMS COMPANY II, L.P., and, GARY PROCTOR Motion to Transfer Venue from the San Francisco Superior Court to District Court of Churchill County, Nevada. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Defendant fails to overcome the presumption that venue is proper in the county where plaintiff filed the claim. There is no evidence to support a discretionary transfer.
